title: Novel cell junction targeting signal peptides and uses thereof
abstract: Provided for herein are novel signal peptides that direct an encoded protein to cell junctions, recombinant polypeptides comprising the same, nucleic acid molecules encoding the same, pharmaceutical compositions comprising the same, and methods of use thereof.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1 . A signal peptide comprising an amino acid sequence selected from the group consisting of Formula I, II, III, IV, V, and VI; wherein Formula I is represented as: A1-A2-A3-A4-A5-A6-A7-A8-A9-A10-A11-A12-A13-A14-A15-A16-A17-A18 (Formula I) and the identities of Ai-Ais are as provided in Table 2; wherein Formula II is represented as: B1-B2-B3-B4-B5-B6-B7-B8-B9-B10-B11-B12-B13-B14-B15-B16-B17-B18-B19 (Formula II) and the identities of B1-B19 are as provided in Table 3; wherein Formula III is represented as: C1-C2-C3-C4-C5-C6-C7-C8-C9-C10-C11-C12-C13-C14-C15-C16-C17-C18-C19-C20-C21-C22 (Formula III) and the identities of C1-C22 are as provided in Table 4; wherein Formula IV is represented as: D1-D2-D3-D4-D5-D6-D7-D8-D9-D10-D11-D12-D13-D14-D15-D16-D17-D18-D19-D20-D21-D22-D23 (Formula IV) and the identities of D1-D23 are as provided in Table 5; wherein Formula V is represented as: E1-E2-E3-E4-E5-E6-E7-E8-E9-E10-E11-E12-E13-E14-E15-E16-E17-E18-E19-E20-E21-E22-E23-E24-E25- E26-E27-E28-E29 (Formula V) and the identities of E1-E29 are as provided in Table 6; and wherein Formula VI is represented as: F1-F2-F3-F4-F5-F6-F7-F8-F9-F 10-F 1 l-F 12-F 13-F 14-F15-F 16-F 17-F18-F I9-F20-F2I-F22-F23-F24-F25-F26- F27-F28-F29-F30-F31 (Formula VI) and the identities of F1-F31 are as provided in Table 7. 2. The signal peptide of claim 1, comprising an amino acid sequence having at least 70%, 75%, 80%, 85%, 86%, 87%, 88%, 89%, 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99%, or 100% identity to an amino acid sequence selected from the group consisting of SEQ ID NO: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7. 3. A signal peptide comprising an amino acid sequence having at least 70%, 75%, 80%, 85%, 86%, 87%, 88%, 89%, 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99%, or 100% identity to an amino acid sequence selected from the group consisting of SEQ ID NO: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7. 4. The signal peptide of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the signal peptide is a novel signal peptide. 5. A recombinant polypeptide comprising a formula of Xi-(Yi) a -Zi, wherein: Xi is a signal peptide, Yi is a peptide linker, and Zi is a payload protein, wherein a is an integer selected from 0 and 1. 6. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 5, wherein Xi comprises an amino acid sequence selected from the group consisting of Formula I, II, III, IV, V, and VI; wherein Formula I is represented as: A1-A2-A3-A4-A5-A6-A7-A8-A9-A10-A11-A12-A13-A14-A15-A16-A17-A18 (Formula I) and the identities of Ai-Ais are as provided in Table 2; wherein Formula II is represented as: B1-B2-B3-B4-B5-B6-B7-B8-B9-B10-B11-B12-B13-B14-B15-B16-B17-B18-B19 (Formula II) and the identities of B1-B19 are as provided in Table 3; wherein Formula III is represented as: C1-C2-C3-C4-C5-C6-C7-C8-C9-C10-C11-C12-C13-C14-C15-C16-C17-C18-C19-C20-C21-C22 (Formula III) and the identities of C1-C22 are as provided in Table 4; wherein Formula IV is represented as: D1-D2-D3-D4-D5-D6-D7-D8-D9-D10-D11-D12-D13-D14-D15-D16-D17-D18-D19-D20-D21-D22-D23 (Formula IV) and the identities of D1-D23 are as provided in Table 5; wherein Formula V is represented as: E1-E2-E3-E4-E5-E6-E7-E8-E9-E10-E11-E12-E13-E14-E15-E16-E17-E18-E19-E20-E21-E22-E23-E24-E25- E26-E27-E28-E29 (Formula V) and the identities of E1-E29 are as provided in Table 6; and 

wherein Formula VI is represented as: F1-F2-F3-F4-F5-F6-F7-F8-F9-F 10-F 1 l-F 12-F 13-F 14-F15-F 16-F 17-F18-F I9-F20-F2I-F22-F23-F24-F25-F26- F27-F28-F29-F30-F31 (Formula VI) and the identities of F1-F31 are as provided in Table 7. 7. The recombinant polypeptide of claim 5 or claim 6, wherein Xi comprises an amino acid sequence having at least 70%, 75%, 80%, 85%, 86%, 87%, 88%, 89%, 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99%, or 100% identity to an amino acid sequence selected from the group consisting of SEQ ID NO: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7. 8. The recombinant polypeptide of any one of claims 5-7, wherein the payload protein is a therapeutic peptide or protein. 9. A nucleic acid molecule encoding for the signal peptide of any one of claims 1-4. 10. A nucleic acid molecule encoding for the recombinant polypeptide of any one of claims 5-8. 11. A vector comprising the nucleic acid molecule of claim 9 or claim 10. 12. A cell comprising the nucleic acid molecule of claim 9 or claim 10 or the vector of claim 11. 13. A composition comprising the nucleic acid molecule of claim 9 or claim 10 or the vector of claim 11. 14. A method for treating a disease or disorder in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of the nucleic acid molecule of claim 10 to the subject, thereby treating the disease or disorder. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the disease or disorder is a cancer. 16. A method for treating a cancer in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering to the subject a vector comprising a nucleic acid molecule encoding for a signal 

peptide fused or linked to a payload protein, wherein the signal peptide is the signal peptide of any one of claims 1-4 and wherein the payload protein is a therapeutic peptide or protein useful for the treatment of the cancer.
